Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

I’ve been testing iOS 27 for a week now and I already wrote about the new Siri AI.
But there’s another feature that honestly impressed me even more than Siri.
Visual Intelligence.
I know that name has been around since iOS 18. But what Apple has done with it in iOS 27 is a completely different level.
I’ve been pointing my camera at everything this week just to see what happens. And most of the time what happens is genuinely impressive.
First Thing It Moved Into the Camera App
In older versions of iOS, Visual Intelligence was kind of hidden.
You had to know where to find it. Most people probably never used it at all.
In iOS 27 Apple moved it right into the Camera app as a dedicated mode called Siri Mode.
It sits right there alongside Photo, Video, and the other camera options. You just swipe over to it like you’d switch to Portrait mode.
